text.title: Python i18n: Babel, FastAPI Middleware, DB Translations
text.summary: Babel setup for Python/Jinja2, FastAPI locale middleware via ContextVar, and database-driven translation model with fallback chain.

section.title: Babel Setup

rule.statement: Cache translation objects with lru_cache; fall back to DEFAULT_LOCALE if the requested locale catalogue is missing.
rule.rationale: Loading translations from disk on every request is expensive. Missing-locale fallback prevents a KeyError from returning a 500 to users in unsupported locales.

section.reference: Babel extraction config for Python + Jinja2 templates

example.code:
from contextvars import ContextVar from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
current_locale: ContextVar[str] = ContextVar("locale", default=DEFAULT_LOCALE)
app = FastAPI()
def get_locale_from_request(request: Request) -> str: # 1. Query param if locale := request.query_params.get("lang"): if locale in SUPPORTED_LOCALES: return locale # 2. Accept-Language header accept = request.headers.get("Accept-Language", "") for lang in accept.split(","): code = lang.split(";")[0].strip()[:2] if code in SUPPORTED_LOCALES: return code return DEFAULT_LOCALE
@app.middleware("http") async def locale_middleware(request: Request, call_next): locale = get_locale_from_request(request) token = current_locale.set(locale) try: response = await call_next(request) response.headers["Content-Language"] = locale return response finally: current_locale.reset(token)

rule.statement: Implement a translation fallback chain: exact locale → base language → default locale → empty string.
rule.rationale: A user with locale uk-UA should fall back to uk before falling back to en. Without this chain, every unsupported sub-locale returns empty strings.
